body_txt: The Anglophone stereotype that France sucks at war and surrenders at the drop of a hat... and needs the Anglophone countries to save them! The idea of France always surrendering might be Newer Than They Think , perhaps only beginning with how quick they surrendered to Nazi Germany during World War II (which itself was due to a combination of more complicated factors, including a fractured government and doctrinal obsolescence, not to mention the French carried on with internal resistance for the rest of the war). This would make it a very specific example of Never Live It Down . The term itself was coined by writer Ken Keeler on an episode of The Simpsons in 1995. This trope is also so widespread because it jibes with that other major negative stereotype Anglophones have about the French — that they're effeminate. It shot back to prominence particularly in US works in 2003 when France refused to support the invasion of Iraq. While many other major nations also voted against it, France for whatever reason came in for a particularly acidic response in conservative works (among other things, they were demands that "French fries" be renamed "freedom fries" note Even though French fries originated in Belgium ). Contrast Gauls with Grenades and Legion of Lost Souls . See also La Résistance . No Real Life Examples, Please! In fact, France is the country which gained the most victories (1115 battles won) in history. Did we mention they also have nuclear weapons? And the most liberal policy for their proactive use? Also it's obvious that insulting France in front of Frenchmen is not a good idea.
